transport.c(3) Library Functions Manual transport.c(3)

NAME

transport.c - WebSocket transport implementation for ACIP protocol.

SYNOPSIS

Macros


#define WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_INCOMING 64
Maximum incoming message queue size (frames received from peer). #define WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_OUTGOING 512
Maximum outgoing message queue size (frames to send to peer). #define WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_INCOMING
#define WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_OUTGOING

Functions


acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_client_transport_create (const char *name, const char *url, crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx)
Create WebSocket client transport. acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_server_transport_create (const char *name, struct lws *wsi, crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx)
Create WebSocket server transport from existing connection.

Detailed Description

WebSocket transport implementation for ACIP protocol.

Implements the acip_transport_t interface for WebSocket connections. Enables browser clients to connect via WebSocket protocol.

ARCHITECTURE:

  • Uses libwebsockets for WebSocket protocol handling
  • Async libwebsockets callbacks bridge to sync recv() via ringbuffer
  • Thread-safe receive queue handles async message arrival
  • Same pattern as WebRTC transport for consistency

MESSAGE FLOW:

1.
send(): Synchronous write via lws_write()
2.
LWS callback: Async write to receive ringbuffer
3.
recv(): Blocking read from receive ringbuffer

MEMORY OWNERSHIP:

  • Transport OWNS wsi (WebSocket instance)
  • Receive queue owns buffered message data
  • recv() allocates message buffer, caller must free

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

February 2026

Definition in file websocket/transport.c.

Macro Definition Documentation

#define WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_INCOMING 64

Maximum incoming message queue size (frames received from peer). Set to 64 for balance: allows buffering across one large frame's fragments while keeping latency reasonable. With ~70 fragments per 291KB frame, we need at least 64 slots to avoid dropping the frame while it's fragmenting.

Definition at line 59 of file websocket/transport.c.

#define WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_OUTGOING 512

Maximum outgoing message queue size (frames to send to peer). Set to 64 to match incoming queue and prevent "BINARY after FIN" errors. Large video frames fragment into ~70 chunks; queue must be large enough to buffer one complete message worth of fragments.

Definition at line 68 of file websocket/transport.c.

#define WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_INCOMING

Definition at line 71 of file websocket/transport.c.

Referenced by acip_websocket_client_transport_create(), and acip_websocket_server_transport_create().

#define WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_OUTGOING

Definition at line 72 of file websocket/transport.c.

Referenced by acip_websocket_client_transport_create(), and acip_websocket_server_transport_create().

Function Documentation

acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_client_transport_create (const char * name, const char * url, crypto_context_t * crypto_ctx)

Create WebSocket client transport.

Parameters

url WebSocket URL (e.g., "ws://localhost:27225")
crypto_ctx Optional encryption context (can be NULL)

Returns

Transport instance or NULL on failure

Definition at line 1366 of file websocket/transport.c.

1367                                                                                        {
1368   if (!name) {
1369     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Transport name is required");
1370     return NULL;
1371   }
1372 
1373   if (!url) {
1374     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "url is required");
1375     return NULL;
1376   }
1377 
1378   // Parse URL to extract host, port, and path
1379   // Format: ws://host:port/path or wss://host:port/path
1380   const char *protocol_end = strstr(url, "://");
1381   if (!protocol_end) {
1382     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid WebSocket URL format (missing ://)");
1383     return NULL;
1384   }
1385 
1386   bool use_ssl = (strncmp(url, "wss://", 6) == 0);
1387   const char *host_start = protocol_end + 3;
1388 
1389   // Find port (if specified)
1390   const char *port_start = strchr(host_start, ':');
1391   const char *path_start = strchr(host_start, '/');
1392 
1393   char host[256] = {0};
1394   int port = use_ssl ? 443 : 27226; // Default: wss:// uses 443, ws:// uses 27226 (ascii-chat WebSocket port)
1395   char path[256] = "/";
1396 
1397   if (port_start && (!path_start || port_start < path_start)) {
1398     // Port is specified
1399     size_t host_len = port_start - host_start;
1400     if (host_len >= sizeof(host)) {
1401       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Host name too long");
1402       return NULL;
1403     }
1404     memcpy(host, host_start, host_len);
1405     host[host_len] = '\0';
1406 
1407     // Extract port
1408     port = atoi(port_start + 1);
1409     if (port <= 0 || port > 65535) {
1410       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid port number");
1411       return NULL;
1412     }
1413   } else {
1414     // No port specified, use default
1415     size_t host_len = path_start ? (size_t)(path_start - host_start) : strlen(host_start);
1416     if (host_len >= sizeof(host)) {
1417       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Host name too long");
1418       return NULL;
1419     }
1420     memcpy(host, host_start, host_len);
1421     host[host_len] = '\0';
1422   }
1423 
1424   // Extract path
1425   if (path_start) {
1426     SAFE_STRNCPY(path, path_start, sizeof(path) - 1);
1427     path[sizeof(path) - 1] = '\0';
1428   }
1429 
1430   log_info("Connecting to WebSocket: %s (host=%s, port=%d, path=%s, ssl=%d)", url, host, port, path, use_ssl);
1431 
1432   // Allocate transport structure
1433   acip_transport_t *transport = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(acip_transport_t), acip_transport_t *);
1434   if (!transport) {
1435     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport");
1436     return NULL;
1437   }
1438 
1439   // Allocate WebSocket-specific data
1440   websocket_transport_data_t *ws_data =
1441       SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(websocket_transport_data_t), websocket_transport_data_t *);
1442   if (!ws_data) {
1443     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1444     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport data");
1445     return NULL;
1446   }
1447 
1448   // Initialize destruction state flag (CALLOC zero-initializes, but explicit for clarity)
1449   atomic_store_bool(&ws_data->is_destroying, false);
1450 
1451   // Create receive queue
1452   ws_data->recv_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_recv_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE);
1453   if (!ws_data->recv_queue) {
1454     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1455     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1456     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create receive queue");
1457     return NULL;
1458   }
1459 
1460   // Create send queue for client transport to buffer outgoing messages
1461   ws_data->send_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE);
1462   if (!ws_data->send_queue) {
1463     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1464     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1465     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1466     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create send queue");
1467     return NULL;
1468   }
1469 
1470   // Initialize synchronization primitives with transport-aware names
1471   char recv_name[64], state_name[64];
1472   snprintf(recv_name, sizeof(recv_name), "recv_%s", name);
1473   snprintf(state_name, sizeof(state_name), "state_%s", name);
1474 
1475   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->recv_mutex, recv_name) != 0) {
1476     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1477     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1478     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1479     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1480     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize recv mutex");
1481     return NULL;
1482   }
1483 
1484   if (cond_init(&ws_data->recv_cond, recv_name) != 0) {
1485     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1486     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1487     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1488     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1489     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1490     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize recv condition variable");
1491     return NULL;
1492   }
1493 
1494   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->state_mutex, state_name) != 0) {
1495     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1496     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1497     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1498     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1499     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1500     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1501     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize state mutex");
1502     return NULL;
1503   }
1504 
1505   if (cond_init(&ws_data->state_cond, state_name) != 0) {
1506     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1507     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1508     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1509     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1510     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1511     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1512     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1513     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize state condition variable");
1514     return NULL;
1515   }
1516 
1517   // Allocate initial send buffer
1518   ws_data->send_buffer_capacity = LWS_PRE + 524288; // Match tx_packet_size (512KB)
1519   ws_data->send_buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(ws_data->send_buffer_capacity, uint8_t *);
1520   if (!ws_data->send_buffer) {
1521     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1522     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1523     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1524     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1525     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1526     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1527     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1528     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate send buffer");
1529     return NULL;
1530   }
1531 
1532   // Create libwebsockets context
1533   // Protocol array must persist for lifetime of context - use static
1534   // NOTE: Define ONLY "acip" protocol for WebSocket connections
1535   // libwebsockets provides built-in "h1" and "http" protocols for WebSocket upgrade handshake.
1536   // When local_protocol_name="h1" and protocol="acip" are specified:
1537   // 1. libwebsockets uses built-in h1 (HTTP/1.1) for initial connection
1538   // 2. HTTP upgrade happens automatically (101 Switching Protocols)
1539   // 3. Connection switches to "acip" protocol using our custom callback
1540   static struct lws_protocols client_protocols[] = {
1541       {
1542           "acip", // ACIP protocol - used after WebSocket upgrade handshake completes
1543           websocket_callback,
1544           0,      // Per-session data - using connect_info.userdata instead
1545           524288, // RX buffer size
1546           0,      // ID
1547           NULL,   // User pointer (set from connect_info.userdata)
1548           524288  // TX packet size
1549       },
1550       {NULL, NULL, 0, 0, 0, NULL, 0} // Terminator
1551   };
1552 
1553   // Disable client compression for now - causes assertion in lws_set_extension_option()
1554   // This is a known issue with libwebsockets permessage-deflate negotiation
1555   // Server-side compression is still enabled, data will be compressed from server to client
1556   // but client->server traffic remains uncompressed (acceptable since client sends less data)
1557   // static const struct lws_extension client_extensions[] = {
1558   //     {"permessage-deflate", lws_extension_callback_pm_deflate, "permessage-deflate; client_max_window_bits=15"},
1559   //     {NULL, NULL, NULL}};
1560 
1561   struct lws_context_creation_info info;
1562   memset(&info, 0, sizeof(info));
1563   info.port = CONTEXT_PORT_NO_LISTEN; // Client mode - no listening
1564   info.protocols = client_protocols;
1565   info.gid = (gid_t)-1; // Cast to avoid undefined behavior with unsigned type
1566   info.uid = (uid_t)-1; // Cast to avoid undefined behavior with unsigned type
1567   // Initialize SSL globally for client (required even for clients to use OpenSSL)
1568   // The LWS_SERVER_OPTION_DO_SSL_GLOBAL_INIT flag works for both servers and clients
1569   info.options = LWS_SERVER_OPTION_DO_SSL_GLOBAL_INIT;
1570   info.extensions = NULL; // Disable client compression due to lws_set_extension_option() assertion
1571 
1572   // Increase per-thread service buffer to prevent fragmentation of large messages
1573   // Default is 4KB, causing 291KB frames to fragment into 73 × 4KB chunks
1574   // Increase to 512KB to match server and allow larger WebSocket frames without fragmentation
1575   info.pt_serv_buf_size = 512 * 1024; // 512KB per-thread service buffer
1576 
1577   // Configure keep-alive to prevent idle disconnects during handshake
1578   // PING every 30 seconds if idle, close after 35 seconds total with no response
1579   static const lws_retry_bo_t client_keep_alive_policy = {
1580       .secs_since_valid_ping = 30,   // Send PING after 30s idle
1581       .secs_since_valid_hangup = 35, // Hangup if still idle after 35s
1582   };
1583   info.retry_and_idle_policy = &client_keep_alive_policy;
1584 
1585   // Enable libwebsockets logging through centralized logging system
1586   lws_log_init_client();
1587 
1588   ws_data->context = lws_create_context(&info);
1589 
1590   if (!ws_data->context) {
1591     SAFE_FREE(ws_data->send_buffer);
1592     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1593     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1594     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1595     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1596     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1597     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1598     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1599     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1600     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to create libwebsockets context");
1601     return NULL;
1602   }
1603 
1604   // Initialize connection state flags BEFORE calling lws_client_connect_via_info
1605   // The callback might fire immediately, so all fields must be ready
1606   ws_data->is_connected = false;      // Will be set to true in LWS_CALLBACK_CLIENT_ESTABLISHED
1607   ws_data->connection_failed = false; // Set to true in LWS_CALLBACK_CLIENT_CONNECTION_ERROR
1608   ws_data->owns_context = true;       // Client transport owns the context
1609 
1610   // Connect to WebSocket server
1611   log_debug("Initiating WebSocket connection to %s:%d%s", host, port, path);
1612   struct lws_client_connect_info connect_info;
1613   memset(&connect_info, 0, sizeof(connect_info));
1614   connect_info.context = ws_data->context;
1615   connect_info.address = host;
1616   connect_info.port = port;
1617   connect_info.path = path;
1618   connect_info.host = host;
1619   connect_info.origin = host;
1620   // Use h1 (HTTP/1.1) protocol for WebSocket upgrade with acip as the subprotocol
1621   // - local_protocol_name="h1": libwebsockets' built-in HTTP/1.1 handler for WebSocket upgrade
1622   // - protocol="acip": Request this subprotocol from the remote server (Sec-WebSocket-Protocol header)
1623   // libwebsockets internally uses h1 regardless of the protocol name, so we use h1 directly
1624   connect_info.local_protocol_name = "h1";  // libwebsockets built-in HTTP/1.1
1625   connect_info.protocol = "acip";  // Remote subprotocol to request from server
1626   // Use SSL + skip server certificate hostname verification + allow self-signed certs (for development)
1627   connect_info.ssl_connection = use_ssl ? (LCCSCF_USE_SSL | LCCSCF_SKIP_SERVER_CERT_HOSTNAME_CHECK | LCCSCF_ALLOW_SELFSIGNED) : 0;
1628   connect_info.userdata = ws_data;
1629 
1630   log_debug("Calling lws_client_connect_via_info...");
1631   ws_data->wsi = lws_client_connect_via_info(&connect_info);
1632   log_debug("lws_client_connect_via_info returned: %p", (void *)ws_data->wsi);
1633   if (!ws_data->wsi) {
1634     lws_context_destroy(ws_data->context);
1635     SAFE_FREE(ws_data->send_buffer);
1636     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1637     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1638     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1639     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1640     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1641     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1642     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1643     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1644     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to connect to WebSocket server");
1645     return NULL;
1646   }
1647 
1648   // Initialize transport
1649   transport->methods = &websocket_methods;
1650   transport->crypto_ctx = crypto_ctx;
1651   transport->impl_data = ws_data;
1652 
1653   // Start service thread immediately - it will drive TLS handshake with frequent polling
1654   // The service thread checks is_destroying and context validity before calling lws_service,
1655   // so it's safe to start it right away without delays.
1656   ws_data->service_running = true;
1657   if (asciichat_thread_create(&ws_data->service_thread, "ws_service", websocket_service_thread, ws_data) != 0) {
1658     log_error("Failed to create WebSocket service thread");
1659     ws_data->service_running = false;
1660     lws_context_destroy(ws_data->context);
1661     SAFE_FREE(ws_data->send_buffer);
1662     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1663     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1664     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1665     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1666     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1667     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1668     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1669     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1670     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to create service thread");
1671     return NULL;
1672   }
1673   log_debug("WebSocket service thread started");
1674 
1675   // CRITICAL FIX: Do NOT block the main thread waiting for connection!
1676   // This prevents stdin/keyboard input from being processed and causes the client to hang.
1677   // The service thread will establish the connection asynchronously.
1678   // The protocol layer (recv) will detect if connection fails and handle it there.
1679   // Return immediately so the main thread can respond to keyboard input.
1680   log_debug("WebSocket transport created, service thread will establish connection asynchronously");
1681 
1682   // Register transport
1683   NAMED_REGISTER_TRANSPORT(transport, name, NULL);
1684 
1685   // Register websocket implementation data with transport as parent
1686   NAMED_REGISTER_WEBSOCKET_IMPL(ws_data, "impl", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(transport));
1687 
1688   // Return transport immediately - connection will be established by service thread
1689   // If connection fails, recv() will detect is_connected=false and return error
1690   return transport;
1691 }




References asciichat_thread_create(),
    atomic_store_bool(), lws_log_init_client(),
    mutex_destroy(), mutex_init(), ringbuffer_create(),
    ringbuffer_destroy(), WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE, and
    WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE.


Referenced by server_connection_establish(), and
    websocket_client_connect().






acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_server_transport_create
  (const char * name, struct lws * wsi, crypto_context_t * crypto_ctx)


Create WebSocket server transport from existing connection. Wraps
    an already-established libwebsockets connection (from server accept). Used
    by websocket_server module to create transports for incoming clients.




Parameters


wsi Established libwebsockets connection (not
  owned by transport)


crypto_ctx Optional crypto context


Returns


Transport instance or NULL on error


Definition at line 1703 of file
    websocket/transport.c.


1704                                                                                        {
1705   if (!name) {
1706     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Transport name is required");
1707     return NULL;
1708   }
1709 
1710   if (!wsi) {
1711     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid wsi parameter");
1712     return NULL;
1713   }
1714 
1715   // Allocate transport structure
1716   acip_transport_t *transport = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(acip_transport_t), acip_transport_t *);
1717   if (!transport) {
1718     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport");
1719     return NULL;
1720   }
1721 
1722   // Allocate transport-specific data
1723   websocket_transport_data_t *ws_data =
1724       SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(websocket_transport_data_t), websocket_transport_data_t *);
1725   if (!ws_data) {
1726     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1727     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport data");
1728     return NULL;
1729   }
1730 
1731   // Initialize receive queue
1732   ws_data->recv_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_recv_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE);
1733   if (!ws_data->recv_queue) {
1734     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1735     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1736     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create receive queue");
1737     return NULL;
1738   }
1739 
1740   // Initialize send queue (for server-side transports)
1741   ws_data->send_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_recv_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE);
1742   if (!ws_data->send_queue) {
1743     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1744     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1745     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1746     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create send queue");
1747     return NULL;
1748   }
1749 
1750   // Initialize pending-free queue for deferred buffer freeing
1751   // permessage-deflate compression holds buffer references asynchronously,
1752   // so we defer freeing to prevent use-after-free errors
1753   ws_data->pending_free_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(pending_free_item_t), 256);
1754   if (!ws_data->pending_free_queue) {
1755     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1756     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1757     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1758     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1759     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create pending-free queue");
1760     return NULL;
1761   }
1762 
1763   // Initialize synchronization primitives with transport-aware names
1764   char recv_name[64], send_name[64], state_name[64], pending_free_name[64];
1765   snprintf(recv_name, sizeof(recv_name), "recv_%s", name);
1766   snprintf(send_name, sizeof(send_name), "send_%s", name);
1767   snprintf(state_name, sizeof(state_name), "state_%s", name);
1768   snprintf(pending_free_name, sizeof(pending_free_name), "pending_free_%s", name);
1769 
1770   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->recv_mutex, recv_name) != 0) {
1771     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1772     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1773     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1774     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1775     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize recv mutex");
1776     return NULL;
1777   }
1778 
1779   if (cond_init(&ws_data->recv_cond, recv_name) != 0) {
1780     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1781     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1782     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1783     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1784     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1785     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize recv condition variable");
1786     return NULL;
1787   }
1788 
1789   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->send_mutex, send_name) != 0) {
1790     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1791     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1792     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1793     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1794     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1795     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1796     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize send mutex");
1797     return NULL;
1798   }
1799 
1800   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->state_mutex, state_name) != 0) {
1801     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1802     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1803     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1804     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1805     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1806     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1807     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1808     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize state mutex");
1809     return NULL;
1810   }
1811 
1812   if (cond_init(&ws_data->state_cond, state_name) != 0) {
1813     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1814     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1815     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1816     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1817     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1818     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1819     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1820     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1821     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize state condition variable");
1822     return NULL;
1823   }
1824 
1825   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->pending_free_mutex, pending_free_name) != 0) {
1826     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1827     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1828     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1829     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1830     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1831     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1832     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1833     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->pending_free_queue);
1834     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1835     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1836     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize pending-free mutex");
1837     return NULL;
1838   }
1839 
1840   // Allocate send buffer with LWS_PRE padding
1841   size_t initial_capacity = 4096 + LWS_PRE;
1842   ws_data->send_buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(initial_capacity, uint8_t *);
1843   if (!ws_data->send_buffer) {
1844     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1845     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1846     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1847     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1848     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1849     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1850     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1851     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1852     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate send buffer");
1853     return NULL;
1854   }
1855   ws_data->send_buffer_capacity = initial_capacity;
1856 
1857   // Store connection info (server-side: no context ownership, connection already established)
1858   ws_data->wsi = wsi;
1859   ws_data->context = lws_get_context(wsi); // Get context from wsi (not owned)
1860   ws_data->owns_context = false;           // Server owns context, not transport
1861   ws_data->is_connected = true;            // Already connected (server-side)
1862   log_info("[WEBSOCKET_TRANSPORT_CREATE] ★★★ SERVER TRANSPORT CREATED: is_connected=true, wsi=%p, ws_data=%p",
1863            (void *)wsi, (void *)ws_data);
1864   log_debug("Server transport created: is_connected=true, wsi=%p", (void *)wsi);
1865 
1866   // Initialize transport
1867   transport->methods = &websocket_methods;
1868   transport->crypto_ctx = crypto_ctx;
1869   transport->impl_data = ws_data;
1870 
1871   log_info("Created WebSocket server transport (crypto: %s)", crypto_ctx ? "enabled" : "disabled");
1872 
1873   // Register transport
1874   NAMED_REGISTER_TRANSPORT(transport, name, NULL);
1875 
1876   // Register websocket implementation data with transport as parent
1877   NAMED_REGISTER_WEBSOCKET_IMPL(ws_data, "impl", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(transport));
1878 
1879   return transport;
1880 }






References mutex_destroy(), mutex_init(),
    ringbuffer_create(), ringbuffer_destroy(),
    WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE, and WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE.








Author


Generated automatically by Doxygen for ascii-chat from the source
    code.







  

    Version 0.9.0
    ascii-chat
